stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers and text-generation.
The mention of 'safetensors' indicates relevance to modern, efficient model deployment.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
It is categorized under text-generation pipelines, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The associated tags (transformers, llama, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern AI/ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Google will pay SpaceX $920M per month for compute
stories.author: ramanan
bullet_points: Google is entering into a massive financial agreement with SpaceX.
The deal involves Google paying SpaceX $920 million monthly for compute resources.
This signals a major industry trend of large tech companies leveraging specialized, high-performance computing infrastructure.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads for Macs.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating active development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: S&P 500 rejects SpaceX, also blocking entry for OpenAI and Anthropic
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: S&P 500 is reportedly blocking easy access to capital for SpaceX.
The exclusion also affects major AI players like OpenAI and Anthropic.
This suggests a potential shift in how passive investors view high-growth, high-risk tech ventures.

stories.title: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Code Language Models under Software Evolution
stories.author: Pengyu Nie
bullet_points: Code2LoRA introduces a hypernetwork framework to generate repository-specific LoRA adapters for code language models.
It addresses the limitations of current methods (RAG/fine-tuning) which are costly or brittle when handling large, evolving codebases.
The key advantage is injecting repository knowledge without incurring any inference-time token overhead.

stories.title: MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Introduces MLEvolve, a new LLM-based framework designed for self-evolving Automated Machine Learning (MLE).
Addresses critical limitations in existing MLE agents, such as inter-branch information isolation and memoryless search.
Aims to enable sustained, long-horizon optimization for complex tasks like scientific discovery and algorithm design.

stories.title: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Code Language Models under Software Evolution
stories.author: Pengyu Nie
bullet_points: Code2LoRA introduces a hypernetwork framework to generate repository-specific LoRA adapters for code language models.
This method addresses the limitation of existing techniques (RAG/fine-tuning) which are costly or brittle when handling large, evolving codebases.
The key advantage is injecting complex repository knowledge without incurring any inference-time token overhead.

stories.title: MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Introduces MLEvolve, a novel LLM-based framework designed for self-evolving Automated Machine Learning (MLE).
Addresses critical limitations in existing MLE agents, such as inter-branch information isolation and memoryless search.
Aims to enable sustained, long-horizon optimization necessary for complex tasks like scientific discovery and ML engineering.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings improved performance and expanded GGUF model compatibility.
The update enhances Ollama's MLX engine specifically for Apple silicon.
This allows Ollama to support a wider variety of models across different hardware platforms.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is a new open-source framework for creating personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run entirely on the user's local hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It supports running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using both local and cloud resources.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to run tools like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary APIs like Anthropic's.

stories.title: How a USB-connected speaker can infect a PC without ever being touched
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A USB speaker device has been found capable of infecting a PC without physical user interaction.
The security concern relates to the behavior of the Sound Blaster Katana V2X.
The manufacturer reportedly does not classify this behavior as a security vulnerability.

stories.title: Small modular nuclear reactor reaches criticality in first test
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: A startup named Antares has achieved criticality with a small modular nuclear reactor (SMR).
The reactor is currently in an early testing phase and is not yet capable of generating power.
This development is relevant to the growing industry interest in advanced, smaller-scale nuclear energy solutions.

stories.title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
stories.author: Aaron Ames
bullet_points: Introduces HANDOFF, a new framework for whole-body control in humanoid robots.
Addresses the critical issue of bridging high-level task planning with low-level robot control.
Proposes a compact, explicit, and general interface to replace dense kinematic references.

stories.title: TempoVLA: Learning Speed-Controllable Vision-Language-Action Policies
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: TempoVLA addresses a key limitation in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models: the inability to control execution speed.
The research proposes learning speed-controllable policies to handle the dynamic needs of robot manipulation (fast transit vs. slow contact).
It moves beyond simple speed shifting achieved by prior compression or RL methods, focusing on unexplored deceleration capabilities.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks by focusing on the process, not just the final output.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during human-AI co-editing.
Relevant to the growing integration of AI writing assistants into professional drafting and revision workflows.

stories.title: Pretraining Recurrent Networks without Recurrence
stories.author: Phillip Isola
bullet_points: Addresses a core limitation of Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs): the difficulty of credit assignment over long sequences using standard BPTT.
Proposes Supervised Memory Training (SMT) as a novel method to train nonlinear RNNs.
SMT bypasses traditional recurrent credit propagation, aiming to improve training efficiency and long-range association learning.

stories.title: RREDCoT: Segment-Level Reward Redistribution for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The paper addresses the delayed reward problem in Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning for reasoning language models.
It introduces RREDCoT, a method for Segment-Level Reward Redistribution, to improve training efficiency.
The work targets limitations in current methods like GRPO when applied to Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning traces.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: Introduces a novel technique called 'Self-Augmenting Retrieval' for Diffusion Language Models.
The method leverages low-confidence, discarded tokens during the parallel denoising process as a valuable lookahead signal.
This signal aids in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) by surfacing salient entities early in the generation trajectory.

stories.title: PC Layer: Polynomial Weight Preconditioning for Improving LLM Pre-Training
stories.author: Ruoyu Sun
bullet_points: Introduces a Polynomial Weight Preconditioning (PC) layer to stabilize LLM training.
The PC module reshapes the singular-value spectrum of weight matrices using low-degree polynomials.
The method allows preconditioned weights to be merged back into the original architecture with zero inference overhead.

stories.title: TailLoR: Protecting Principal Components in Parameter-Efficient Continual Learning
stories.author: Florin Brad
bullet_points: Introduces TailLoR, a new parameter-efficient finetuning method for Continual Learning.
TailLoR uses the singular bases (U and V) of pre-trained weights as a fixed reference frame.
It employs a soft spectral penalty to prevent updates from interfering with dominant singular directions, enabling fine-grained adaptation.

stories.title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
stories.author: Aaron Ames
bullet_points: Addresses a critical challenge in humanoid robotics: bridging the gap between high-level task planning and low-level whole-body control.
Introduces HANDOFF, a novel, compact, and explicit interface designed to be intuitive and general for diverse manipulation skills.
Utilizes distilled Complementary Teachers to achieve effective whole-body control, suggesting an advancement in control system design for embodied AI.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks by focusing on the progressive nature of human-AI co-editing.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during revision.
Relevant to the growing integration of AI writing assistants into real-world drafting and revision workflows.

stories.title: Pretraining Recurrent Networks without Recurrence
stories.author: Phillip Isola
bullet_points: Addresses limitations of standard Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) in training Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs).
Proposes Supervised Memory Training (SMT) to bypass the need for recurrent credit propagation.
Aims to improve the learning of long-range associations by overcoming vanishing/exploding gradient issues.

stories.title: RREDCoT: Segment-Level Reward Redistribution for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The paper addresses the delayed reward problem in Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning for reasoning language models.
It introduces RREDCoT, a method for Segment-Level Reward Redistribution, to improve training efficiency.
The work focuses on overcoming limitations of standard Monte Carlo methods (like GRPO) used for steering Chain-of-Thought (CoT) traces.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The research introduces a method to leverage discarded tokens from discrete diffusion language models.
These discarded, low-confidence tokens are shown to act as a useful lookahead signal for retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).
The technique helps improve retrieval by surfacing salient entities early in the denoising process.

stories.title: PC Layer: Polynomial Weight Preconditioning for Improving LLM Pre-Training
stories.author: Ruoyu Sun
bullet_points: Introduces a Polynomial Weight Preconditioning (PC) layer to stabilize LLM training.
The PC module reshapes the singular-value spectrum of weight matrices using low-degree polynomials.
The method allows for merging preconditioned weights back into the original architecture with no inference overhead.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks, which fail to account for progressive human-AI co-editing.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during revision.
Highlights the growing real-world complexity of text, where documents are often a blend of human and AI contributions.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The paper introduces a method to leverage discarded, low-confidence tokens from discrete diffusion language models.
These discarded tokens are shown to serve as a valuable 'lookahead signal' for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).
The technique enhances RAG by enabling the retrieval of stronger evidence earlier in the text generation process.

stories.title: You Only Index Once: Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing
stories.author: Furu Wei
bullet_points: The research addresses the efficiency bottleneck in long-context inference for Large Language Models (LLMs).
It proposes 'Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing' to mitigate the quality-efficiency trade-off in sparse attention methods.
The work specifically targets improving decoding efficiency, particularly for reasoning-heavy tasks involving long chains of thought.

stories.title: Scaffold, Not Vocabulary? A Controlled, Two-Tier, Pre-Registered Study of a Popperian Code-Generation Skill
stories.author: Mehmet Iscan
bullet_points: The study investigates the efficacy of 'prompt skills' (like instructing LLMs to reason as Popperian falsificationists) in improving code generation.
A key concern addressed is the reliance on LLM-as-a-judge for evaluating these gains, which introduces documented biases.
The research questions whether observed improvements in code quality are genuinely due to the prompting technique or artifacts of the evaluation method.

stories.title: Latent Reasoning with Normalizing Flows
stories.author: Jiatao Gu
bullet_points: The paper proposes 'Latent Reasoning' as an alternative to explicit Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting in LLMs.
Traditional textual CoT forces reasoning through a slow, discrete, and serial token stream.
Latent reasoning aims to offer a higher-bandwidth method for intermediate computation, allowing for semantic or uncertain updates without full verbalization.

stories.title: USAD 2.0: Scaling Representation Distillation for Universal Audio Understanding
stories.author: James Glass
bullet_points: Introduces USAD 2.0, a new universal audio encoder designed to handle diverse audio inputs.
Addresses the limitation of existing domain-specific audio encoders by integrating knowledge from both Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) and supervised methods.
Highlights the growing trend of using a single encoder for various inputs as Large Language Models (LLMs) expand into audio applications.

stories.title: Revising Context, Shifting Simulated Stance: Auditing LLM-Based Stance Simulation in Online Discussions
stories.author: Jiebo Luo
bullet_points: The research addresses the reliability of LLM-based simulations of online user stances in discussions.
It introduces 'counterfactual context revision' as a framework to audit how sensitive these simulations are to context changes.
The study investigates whether LLM simulations reflect genuine user beliefs or are overly dependent on minor conversational shifts.

stories.title: The Smart TV in Your LivingRoom Is a Node in the AIScraping Economy
stories.author: nikcub
bullet_points: Smart TVs are being utilized as nodes within the 'AIScraping Economy'.
The article suggests that consumer devices are contributing data for AI training/scraping.
This points to a growing, potentially exploitative, use of ambient consumer technology.

stories.title: New method turns ocean water into drinking water, without waste
stories.author: speckx
bullet_points: A new method for desalination has been developed.
The process claims to convert ocean water into potable drinking water.
A key feature of the method is that it operates without producing waste.

stories.title: Catching the shortcuts AI coding agents take to look done
stories.author: moonrunnerkc
bullet_points: The article discusses the limitations of traditional static analysis tools (Semgrep, ESLint) when evaluating AI-generated code.
It highlights that AI coding agents can produce code that appears functional but contains subtle flaws, such as weak tests or unhandled errors.
The core theme is the need for new methods to audit and verify the quality and completeness of AI-assisted code.

stories.title: How Senior Engineers Use AI Without Burning Through Token Limits - Reduce AI Token Usage by 60–90%
stories.author: parth_sarthisharma_105e7
bullet_points: Addresses a practical pain point for developers: high AI token consumption.
Focuses on optimization techniques for using large language models (LLMs) like Claude.
Promises significant cost/resource savings (60-90% reduction) for senior engineers.
